words.def: immediately
words.example: Her voice is instantly recognizable.

words.def: an organization that has a particular purpose, especially one that is connected with education or a particular profession; the building used by this organization
words.example: The report was compiled by Germany's five leading economic research institutes.

words.def: a large important organization that has a particular purpose, for example a university or bank
words.example: The deal is backed by one of the country's largest financial institutions.

words.def: an arrangement with a company in which you pay them regular amounts of money and they agree to pay the costs, for example, if you die or are ill, or if you lose or damage something
words.example: health/medical insurance

words.def: to combine two or more things so that they work together; to combine with something else in this way
words.example: integrate into/with something, These programs will integrate with your existing software.

words.def: that you are trying to achieve or reach
words.example: the intended purpose

words.def: very great; very strong
words.example: We were all suffering in the intense heat.

words.def: to communicate with somebody, especially while you work, play or spend time with them
words.example: Teachers have a limited amount of time to interact with each child.

words.def: the act of communicating with somebody, especially while you work, play or spend time with them
words.example: the interaction between performers and their audience

words.def: connected with the inside of something
words.example: the internal structure of a building

words.def: to explain the meaning of something
words.example: The students were asked to interpret the poem.

words.def: the particular way in which something is understood or explained
words.example: Her evidence suggests a different interpretation of the events.

words.def: to say or do something that makes somebody stop what they are saying or doing
words.example: Sorry to interrupt, but there's someone to see you.

words.def: a period of time between two events
words.example: The interval between major earthquakes might be 200 years.

words.def: to enter a country, town, etc. using military force in order to take control of it
words.example: Troops invaded on August 9th that year.

words.def: the act of an army entering another country by force in order to take control of it
words.example: the German invasion of Poland in 1939

words.def: to carefully examine the facts of a situation, an event, a crime, etc. to find out the truth about it or how it happened
words.example: The FBI has been called in to investigate.

words.def: an official examination of the facts about a situation, crime, etc.
words.example: a criminal/murder/police investigation

words.def: the act of investing money in something
words.example: foreign/private investment

words.def: a person or an organization that invests money in something
words.example: small investors (= private people)

words.def: to separate somebody/something physically or socially from other people or things
words.example: isolate somebody/yourself/something, Patients with the disease should be isolated.

words.def: far away from any others
words.example: isolated rural areas

words.def: an important topic that people are discussing or arguing about
words.example: a key/major issue

words.def: a prison
words.example: She spent a year in jail.

words.def: a plane driven by jet engines
words.example: a jet aircraft/fighter/airliner

words.def: involving two or more people together
words.example: a joint account (= a bank account in the name of more than one person, for example shared by a couple)

words.def: the work of collecting and writing news stories for newspapers, magazines, radio, television or online news sites; the news stories that are written
words.example: I'd like a career in journalism.

words.def: a feeling of great happiness
words.example: Her books have brought joy to millions.

words.def: the ability to make sensible decisions after carefully considering the best thing to do
words.example: good/poor/sound judgement

words.def: an act of jumping
words.example: to do a parachute jump

words.def: having a low rank in an organization or a profession
words.example: a junior partner/minister/officer

words.def: a group of members of the public who listen to the facts of a case in a court and decide whether or not somebody is guilty of a crime, or whether a claim has been proved
words.example: members of the jury

words.def: the fair treatment of people
words.example: She spent her life fighting for social justice and equality for women.

words.def: to show that somebody/something is right or reasonable
words.example: justify doing something, How can they justify paying such huge salaries?

words.def: wanting to do something or wanting something to happen very much
words.example: keen to do something, John was very keen to help.

words.def: a set of tools or equipment that you use for a particular purpose
words.example: a first-aid kit

words.def: a piece of paper, etc. that is attached to something and that gives information about it
words.example: Always read the label carefully.

words.def: work, especially physical work
words.example: manual labour (= work using your hands)

words.def: a piece of equipment for climbing up and down a wall, the side of a building, etc., consisting of two lengths of wood or metal that are joined together by steps or rungs
words.example: to climb up/fall off a ladder

words.def: an act of bringing an aircraft or a spacecraft down to the ground after a journey
words.example: a perfect/smooth/safe landing

words.def: everything you can see when you look across a large area of land, especially in the country
words.example: the woods and fields that are typical features of the English landscape

words.def: a narrow road in the country
words.example: winding country lanes

words.def: a small computer that can work with a battery and be easily carried
words.example: I opened my laptop and started typing.

words.def: to a great extent; mostly or mainly
words.example: The manager was largely responsible for the team’s victory.

words.def: recently; in the recent past
words.example: Have you seen her lately?

words.def: coming after something else or at a time in the future
words.example: Rembrandt's later works

words.def: the most recent or newest
words.example: the latest craze/fashion/trend

words.def: the action of launching something
words.example: a missile/rocket launch

words.def: the state or position of being a leader
words.example: a leadership role/position

words.def: a printed sheet of paper or a few printed pages that are given free to advertise or give information about something
words.example: We picked up a few leaflets on local places of interest.

words.def: a group of sports teams who all play each other to earn points and find which team is best
words.example: Castleford have led the league for most of the season.

words.def: to bend or move from a straight position to a sloping position
words.example: I leaned back in my chair.

words.def: the process of learning something
words.example: lifelong/adult learning

words.def: a period of time when you are allowed to be away from work for a holiday or for a special reason
words.example: to take a month’s paid/unpaid leave

words.def: a story from ancient times about people and events, that may or may not be true; this type of story
words.example: The film is based on the legend of Robin Hood.
